description:
## Goal
Introduce `BranchOrigin` and a side-table on `module.source` recording where each generated `Term::If` came from. Data model only — no behaviour change.

## Why
Today three independent mechanisms try to answer "is this If user-written or generated machinery?": `is_synthetic_match_error_block`, the absence of a source span, and the shape of the dead block. They disagree on intermediate failure-funnel blocks. One source of truth instead.

## Shape
Mirrors `module.source.term_span`:

```rust
enum BranchOrigin { User, PatternBind, ClauseDispatch, ParamGuard }
module.source.branch_origins: HashMap<(FnId, BlockId), BranchOrigin>
```

Absence = `User`. BlockIds are stable across reducer/fuse/DCE (no renumbering), so the key is safe.

## ir_lower sites to tag
- `lower_expr` → `Expr::Match` → the `If` at the dispatch (`PatternBind`).
- `lower_pattern_matrix` and friends → every `If` introduced by pattern-matrix dispatch (`PatternBind` when called from `Expr::Match` lowering; `ClauseDispatch` when called from fn-clause lowering).
- `emit_param_type_guards` → param-type-guard `If`s (`ParamGuard`).

Pass the origin into a small helper that wraps `ctx.set_term(Term::If(...))` so it's set at the same place the If is emitted; no risk of forgetting.

## Out of scope
- Producers and consumers of `dead_branches` (E2, E3, E4).

## Done when
- `BranchOrigin` enum + side-table land in `fz_ir`.
- Every `Term::If` generated by lowering machinery has its origin tagged.
- A unit test asserts the four origin kinds for a small program covering each lowering path.
- Existing tests pass unchanged.
title: E1: BranchOrigin side-table — ir_lower tags every generated Term::If
